---
toc: true
---
<div class="hero">
<div class="kicker">
APPENDIX · GLOSSARY
</div>
# Glossary
<div class="lead">
Plain-English definitions of the terms this curriculum uses the most.
</div>
</div>

Each entry links to the first lab in which the term appears.

## A

**α (alpha).** The probability of a type I error — rejecting a true null hypothesis. Conventional values are 0.05 and 0.01. [Hypothesis testing p values type i ii errors](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-hypothesis-testing-p-values-type-i-ii-errors.Rmd).

**ANCOVA.** Analysis of covariance: a linear model combining categorical predictors and continuous covariates, commonly used to adjust RCT analyses for baseline. [Ancova in rcts baseline adjustment](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-ancova-in-rcts-baseline-adjustment.Rmd).

**ANOVA.** Analysis of variance: a linear model with categorical predictors. [One way anova and contrasts emmeans](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-one-way-anova-and-contrasts-emmeans.Rmd).

**Assumption.** A condition the chosen test needs to be interpretable — normality, equal variance, independence, linearity. [Two sample and paired t tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-sample-and-paired-t-tests.Rmd).

## B

**Bias.** Systematic deviation of an estimator from the quantity being estimated. [Populations samples and the central limit theorem](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-populations-samples-and-the-central-limit-theorem.Rmd).

**Bootstrap.** Resampling with replacement to approximate the sampling distribution of a statistic. [Bootstrap and permutation tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-bootstrap-and-permutation-tests.Rmd).

**Brier score.** Mean squared error between predicted probabilities and observed outcomes. [Calibration discrimination roc auc brier score](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-calibration-discrimination-roc-auc-brier-score.Rmd).

## C

**CI (confidence interval).** A range of plausible values for a parameter; in repeated sampling, 95% CIs cover the true value 95% of the time. [Bootstrap and permutation tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-bootstrap-and-permutation-tests.Rmd).

**Cohen's d.** Standardised difference in means; a common effect size for two-group comparisons. [Two sample and paired t tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-sample-and-paired-t-tests.Rmd).

**Competing risk.** An event whose occurrence precludes or alters the probability of the event of interest. [Competing risks and multistate models](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-competing-risks-and-multistate-models.Rmd).

**Confounding.** A third variable distorting the association between exposure and outcome. [Multiple regression confounding interaction centring](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-multiple-regression-confounding-interaction-centring.Rmd).

**Cox model.** A proportional-hazards regression for time-to-event outcomes. [Survival primer km log rank cox ph](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-survival-primer-km-log-rank-cox-ph.Rmd).

**CV (cross-validation).** Splitting data into folds to estimate generalisation error. [Cross validation nested cv bootstrap 632](../chapters/13-ml/labs/lab-cross-validation-nested-cv-bootstrap-632.Rmd).

## D

**DAG (directed acyclic graph).** A graphical representation of causal assumptions. [Dags with dagitty and ggdag](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-dags-with-dagitty-and-ggdag.Rmd).

## E

**Effect size.** A standardised measure of the magnitude of an effect, independent of sample size. [Two sample and paired t tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-sample-and-paired-t-tests.Rmd).

## F

**FDR (false discovery rate).** The expected proportion of false positives among rejected nulls. [Fdr knockoffs replication crisis](../chapters/13-ml/labs/lab-fdr-knockoffs-replication-crisis.Rmd).

**Fisher's exact test.** Exact test of independence for 2×2 tables, appropriate when expected cell counts are small. [Two proportions chi square risk and odds](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-proportions-chi-square-risk-and-odds.Rmd).

## G

**GAM (generalised additive model).** A regression with smooth non-linear terms, fitted via penalised splines. [Gams with mgcv](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-gams-with-mgcv.Rmd).

**GEE (generalised estimating equations).** A marginal-model approach for clustered or repeated data. [Glmms and gee](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-glmms-and-gee.Rmd).

**GLM (generalised linear model).** A regression with a link function and exponential-family error. [Logistic regression binomial glm](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-logistic-regression-binomial-glm.Rmd).

## H

**Hazard ratio.** Ratio of hazard rates between two groups in a survival model. [Survival primer km log rank cox ph](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-survival-primer-km-log-rank-cox-ph.Rmd).

## I

**ICC (intraclass correlation).** Proportion of variance attributable to clustering. [Kappa icc bland altman](../chapters/14-clinical/labs/lab-kappa-icc-bland-altman.Rmd).

**Interaction.** The effect of one predictor depends on the value of another. [Multiple regression confounding interaction centring](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-multiple-regression-confounding-interaction-centring.Rmd).

**IPTW (inverse-probability-of-treatment weighting).** Propensity-score method that reweights observations to emulate a trial. [Propensity scores and iptw](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-propensity-scores-and-iptw.Rmd).

## K

**Kaplan-Meier.** Non-parametric estimator of the survival function. [Survival primer km log rank cox ph](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-survival-primer-km-log-rank-cox-ph.Rmd).

**Kruskal-Wallis.** Non-parametric one-way ANOVA on ranks. [Non parametric tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-non-parametric-tests.Rmd).

## L

**Lasso.** L1-regularised regression; produces sparse coefficient estimates. [Regularisation ridge lasso elastic net](../chapters/13-ml/labs/lab-regularisation-ridge-lasso-elastic-net.Rmd).

**Likelihood.** The probability of the observed data under a model, viewed as a function of the parameters. [Maximum likelihood estimation](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-maximum-likelihood-estimation.Rmd).

**Linear model.** A model of the form $y = X\beta + \varepsilon$. [Simple linear regression](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-simple-linear-regression.Rmd).

**Logistic regression.** GLM with a logit link for binary outcomes. [Logistic regression binomial glm](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-logistic-regression-binomial-glm.Rmd).

## M

**MAR (missing at random).** Missingness depends on observed variables only. [Mcar mar mnar](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-mcar-mar-mnar.Rmd).

**MCAR (missing completely at random).** Missingness is independent of all variables. [Mcar mar mnar](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-mcar-mar-mnar.Rmd).

**MCMC.** Markov-chain Monte Carlo; the Bayesian posterior-sampling workhorse. [Brms stan loo hierarchical models](../chapters/10-bayesian/labs/lab-brms-stan-loo-hierarchical-models.Rmd).

**MDE (minimum detectable effect).** Smallest effect your study has power to detect. [Power closed form and simulation](../chapters/05-power/labs/lab-power-closed-form-and-simulation.Rmd).

**Meta-analysis.** Combining effect estimates across studies. [Meta analysis basics](../chapters/15-metaanalysis/labs/lab-meta-analysis-basics.Rmd).

**Mixed model.** Regression combining fixed and random effects. [Linear mixed models with lme4](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-linear-mixed-models-with-lme4.Rmd).

**MNAR (missing not at random).** Missingness depends on unobserved values. [Mcar mar mnar](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-mcar-mar-mnar.Rmd).

**Multiple imputation.** Imputing missing values several times and pooling the analyses. [Multiple imputation with mice](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-multiple-imputation-with-mice.Rmd).

## N

**Non-parametric.** A test or estimator that makes weak distributional assumptions. [Non parametric tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-non-parametric-tests.Rmd).

## O

**Odds ratio.** Ratio of odds between two groups; the natural scale for logistic regression. [Logistic regression binomial glm](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-logistic-regression-binomial-glm.Rmd).

**Outlier.** An observation far from the bulk of the data. [Diagnostics residuals qq leverage cook s distance vif](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-diagnostics-residuals-qq-leverage-cook-s-distance-vif.Rmd).

**Overdispersion.** Variance exceeding the model's nominal variance; common in Poisson regression. [Poisson and negative binomial regression](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-poisson-and-negative-binomial-regression.Rmd).

## P

**p-value.** Probability of data as or more extreme than observed, assuming the null. [Hypothesis testing p values type i ii errors](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-hypothesis-testing-p-values-type-i-ii-errors.Rmd).

**Paired test.** A test comparing matched observations rather than independent samples. [Two sample and paired t tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-sample-and-paired-t-tests.Rmd).

**PCA (principal components analysis).** Linear dimension reduction by orthogonal projection onto directions of maximum variance. [Pca factor analysis cca lda](../chapters/08-multivariate/labs/lab-pca-factor-analysis-cca-lda.Rmd).

**Permutation test.** Inference by shuffling labels to build a null distribution. [Bootstrap and permutation tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-bootstrap-and-permutation-tests.Rmd).

**Poisson regression.** GLM with a log link for counts. [Poisson and negative binomial regression](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-poisson-and-negative-binomial-regression.Rmd).

**Power.** Probability of detecting an effect if it exists; 1 − β. [Sample size power and quarto reporting](../chapters/05-power/labs/lab-sample-size-power-and-quarto-reporting.Rmd).

**Pre-registration.** A timestamped record of the research plan before data analysis. [Pre registration and statistical analysis plans](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-pre-registration-and-statistical-analysis-plans.Rmd).

**Propensity score.** Probability of treatment given covariates. [Propensity scores and iptw](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-propensity-scores-and-iptw.Rmd).

**Pseudoreplication.** Treating correlated observations as independent replicates. [Bench and translational design](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-bench-and-translational-design.Rmd).

## R

**Random effect.** A model coefficient treated as a draw from a distribution. [Linear mixed models with lme4](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-linear-mixed-models-with-lme4.Rmd).

**Randomisation.** Allocating units to arms by a chance mechanism. [Randomised controlled trials](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-randomised-controlled-trials.Rmd).

**Regression to the mean.** Tendency of extreme values to be closer to the mean on remeasurement. [Dichotomisation change scores regression to the mean](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-dichotomisation-change-scores-regression-to-the-mean.Rmd).

**Reliability.** Consistency of repeated measurements. [Kappa icc bland altman](../chapters/14-clinical/labs/lab-kappa-icc-bland-altman.Rmd).

**Resampling.** Bootstrap, permutation, and cross-validation collectively. [Bootstrap and permutation tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-bootstrap-and-permutation-tests.Rmd).

**Residual.** Observed minus predicted. [Diagnostics residuals qq leverage cook s distance vif](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-diagnostics-residuals-qq-leverage-cook-s-distance-vif.Rmd).

**Risk ratio.** Ratio of event probabilities between two groups. [Two proportions chi square risk and odds](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-proportions-chi-square-risk-and-odds.Rmd).

**Robust SE.** A standard error computed without assuming homoscedasticity. [Robust regression weighted ls hc sandwich standard errors](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-robust-regression-weighted-ls-hc-sandwich-standard-errors.Rmd).

**ROC / AUC.** Receiver operating characteristic; area under it measures discrimination. [Calibration discrimination roc auc brier score](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-calibration-discrimination-roc-auc-brier-score.Rmd).

## S

**SAP (statistical analysis plan).** The formal plan for a trial's analysis, written before data are seen. [Pre registration and statistical analysis plans](../chapters/16-design/labs/lab-pre-registration-and-statistical-analysis-plans.Rmd).

**SE (standard error).** Standard deviation of a sampling distribution. [Populations samples and the central limit theorem](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-populations-samples-and-the-central-limit-theorem.Rmd).

**SEM (standard error of the mean).** Standard error of a sample mean. [Populations samples and the central limit theorem](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-populations-samples-and-the-central-limit-theorem.Rmd).

**Shapiro-Wilk.** A test of normality, powerful in small samples. [Two sample and paired t tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-two-sample-and-paired-t-tests.Rmd).

**Spearman correlation.** Rank-based correlation. [Pearson and spearman correlation](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-pearson-and-spearman-correlation.Rmd).

**Survival.** Time-to-event analysis. [Survival primer km log rank cox ph](../chapters/11-survival/labs/lab-survival-primer-km-log-rank-cox-ph.Rmd).

## T

**Type I / II error.** False-positive and false-negative errors of a test. [Hypothesis testing p values type i ii errors](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-hypothesis-testing-p-values-type-i-ii-errors.Rmd).

## V

**VIF (variance inflation factor).** Measure of collinearity among regression predictors. [Diagnostics residuals qq leverage cook s distance vif](../chapters/07-regression/labs/lab-diagnostics-residuals-qq-leverage-cook-s-distance-vif.Rmd).

## W

**Wilcoxon test.** Non-parametric test for paired (signed-rank) or unpaired (rank-sum) data. [Non parametric tests](../chapters/04-inferential/labs/lab-non-parametric-tests.Rmd).
